title: Osteoarthritis — implied, overclaim, not-supported
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: At week 12, the drug reduced knee pain scores more than placebo.1 The drug significantly improved physical function.1 The drug slowed structural joint damage.2

goldClaims.originalText: At week 12, the drug reduced knee pain scores more than placebo.1
goldClaims.text: At week 12, the drug reduced knee pain scores more than placebo

goldClaims.originalText: The drug significantly improved physical function.1
goldClaims.text: The drug significantly improved physical function

goldClaims.originalText: The drug slowed structural joint damage.2
goldClaims.text: The drug slowed structural joint damage

1.name: OA-RELIEF table (Marsh 2024)
1.text: Table 1. WOMAC pain change at week 12: drug -3.4, placebo -1.6. WOMAC physical-function score change: drug -2.9, placebo -1.4.

2.name: OA-RELIEF imaging
2.text: Structural joint outcomes such as joint-space narrowing were not assessed in this trial.

goldVerdicts.claimText: At week 12, the drug reduced knee pain scores more than placebo
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a data table (WOMAC pain -3.4 vs -1.6) with no narrative sentence (rule 5).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The drug significantly improved physical function
goldVerdicts.rationale: Table shows a function change (-2.9 vs -1.4) but states no significance; claim asserts 'significantly' (rule 1).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The drug slowed structural joint damage
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref says structural outcomes were not assessed — silent (rule 2).
